trade.summary: Spagna and Polonia have growing bilateral trade as major EU economies. Spagna exports food products, vehicles, and chemicals; Polonia exports machinery, furniture, vehicles, and food products. Both benefit from EU single market access. Polonia is Spagna's most important Eastern European trade partner.

diplomatic.status: EU partners with improving relations under Tusk
diplomatic.summary: Spagna and Polonia are EU members with generally good but not always aligned positions. Under the Tusk government (2023-), relations improved significantly as Polonia moved back toward EU mainstream positions. Both cooperate on Ucraina support. They differ somewhat on migration policy (Polonia more restrictive, Spagna more open). Both are NATO allies.

military.summary: Both NATO members. Spagna has contributed to NATO Enhanced Forward Presence missions in Polonia's neighborhood. Polonia is one of NATO's most important eastern flank states with rising defense spending. Both cooperate on Ucraina military assistance within NATO frameworks.

financial.summary: Moderate bilateral investment. Spanish banks (Santander, BBVA) have some Polish operations. Spanish infrastructure firms are active in Polonia (benefiting from EU cohesion funds). Polish investment in Spagna is minimal.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Polonia is coal-dependent and transitioning; Spagna is a renewable leader. Some potential for Spanish renewable electricity (via interconnectors) to eventually reach Polonia. Spagna's LNG expertise could benefit Polish LNG import ambitions.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation through EU Horizon programs. Spanish and Polish universities collaborate in physics, chemistry, and environmental sciences. Large Polish community in Spagna contributes to people-to-people ties. Erasmus program is extensively used between the two countries.
